libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that create or dereference JavaBean References with com.mchange.v2.naming.JavaBeanReferenceMaker or JavaBeanObjectFactory, set the system property `com.mchange.v2.naming.referenceableJavaBeanClassWhitelist` to the fully qualified test bean class name before calling `createReference(...)` or `getObjectInstance(...)`, and restore/clear it in `finally` or `@AfterEach`. If using ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) overloads that do not pass an explicit allowed factory set, also whitelist `com.mchange.v2.naming.JavaBeanObjectFactory` via `com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist` or pass an explicit allowed factory set. Avoid `*` unless intentionally testing the unsafe disabled-whitelist mode.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8716 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.6.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Whitelist configuration is global when set as a system property, so tests must avoid leaking it across test methods.
If both system properties and mchange PropertiesConfig provide a whitelist, the library uses the intersection; conflicting values can still fail.

The artifact declares optional integrations for Typesafe Config, log4j 1.x, log4j2, and slf4j, but these are not required for core coverage and should not be added deterministically unless tests deliberately cover those optional adapters.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.6.0 tightened JNDI JavaBean reference security; tests that exercise JavaBeanReferenceMaker/JavaBeanObjectFactory must explicitly whitelist the test bean class or Java execution fails before reaching meaningful coverage of that path.
